toneGuidelines.defaultTone: Warm, professional, culturally aware - blend international standards with Mauritian hospitality
principles: Use guest's name in every communication
Match formality level to guest's nationality and communication style
Show genuine local knowledge, not generic tourist info

escalationLadder.name: Friendly & Welcoming
escalationLadder.when: Standard communications, happy guests, routine inquiries
escalationLadder.example: We're so excited to host you! Here's a little insider tip for your first evening...

escalationLadder.name: Concerned & Attentive
escalationLadder.when: Minor issues reported, mild dissatisfaction signals, maintenance requests
escalationLadder.example: I'm sorry to hear about that. I've already contacted our maintenance team and they'll be there within the hour. In the meantime, here's what you can do...

escalationLadder.name: Firm & Professional
escalationLadder.when: Repeated issues, house rule violations, unreasonable demands, noise complaints from neighbors
escalationLadder.tone: Direct but respectful, clear boundaries, documented
escalationLadder.example: I understand your perspective and I want to find a solution that works. However, our house rules clearly state... I'd appreciate your cooperation so we can ensure a great experience for everyone.

escalationLadder.name: Final & Documented
escalationLadder.when: Serious violations, safety concerns, potential eviction, legal matters
escalationLadder.example: This is a formal notice regarding [specific violation]. As outlined in our rental agreement, [specific clause]. We require immediate compliance. If this continues, we will need to [specific consequence]. This communication is being documented.

english.style: Varies by nationality - formal for German/Indian, casual for UK/SA

french.style: Formal address (vous), detailed information, cultural appreciation

keyPhrases.welcome: Bienvenue a votre propriete de vacances
keyPhrases.wifi: Reseau WiFi: [nom] Mot de passe: [mot de passe]

bestPractices: Detect guest language from booking platform and initial messages
Send key documents (check-in instructions, emergency info, house rules) in guest's language
Use professional translation for legal and safety documents, AI translation for casual messages
Include visual aids (icons, photos, diagrams) to supplement text for language barriers
QR codes linking to translated versions of property guides
Kreol phrases as cultural touch points enhance authenticity
multilingualHandling.mauritiusAdvantage: English-French bilingual capability serves 80%+ of visitor demographics

french.communicationStyle: Formal address, detailed information, cultural appreciation
french.preferences: Later dining recommendations (8-9 PM), wine knowledge, sophisticated cultural recommendations
french.responsePattern: Prefer comprehensive responses vs brief acknowledgments
tips: Fine dining and art recommendations valued

german.preferences: Detailed guides, clear instructions, punctuality emphasis, efficiency
tips: Provide detailed maps and transport info

british.communicationStyle: Polite, appreciates subtle humor, traditional hospitality
british.preferences: Beach-centric, sun/shade balance, familiar food options alongside local cuisine
british.serviceStyle: Proactive but non-intrusive, respect for privacy

indian.culturalNote: Guest as god cultural expectation = elevated service standards

southAfrican.preferences: Outdoor adventures, water sports, value-for-money emphasis

difficultGuestScenarios.scenario: Guest complains about cleanliness on arrival
difficultGuestScenarios.response: Apologize immediately and sincerely. Send cleaning team within 1 hour. Offer 10-20% service credit. Follow up within 2 hours to confirm satisfaction. Document with photos for quality improvement.
difficultGuestScenarios.prevention: 2-hour pre-arrival inspection window, photo documentation, white sock test on floors

difficultGuestScenarios.scenario: Guest demands early check-in (property not ready)
difficultGuestScenarios.response: Acknowledge request warmly. Explain turnaround timing honestly. Offer luggage storage or recommend nearby cafe/beach. If possible, expedite cleaning. Never promise what you can't deliver.
difficultGuestScenarios.prevention: Set clear check-in windows in pre-arrival messaging (3-8 PM). Offer early check-in as paid upgrade when available.

difficultGuestScenarios.scenario: Noise complaint from neighbors about guests
difficultGuestScenarios.response: Contact guest immediately with friendly but firm reminder of quiet hours. Reference house rules. If persistent, escalate to Level 3 tone. Document all communications for potential platform dispute.
difficultGuestScenarios.prevention: Install privacy-compliant noise monitoring (Minut/NoiseAware). Include noise rules prominently in welcome materials.

difficultGuestScenarios.scenario: Guest threatens negative review to get discount
difficultGuestScenarios.response: Stay professional and documented. Acknowledge their concerns genuinely. Offer reasonable resolution based on actual service failure. Do not cave to threats - document the extortion attempt for platform dispute resolution.
difficultGuestScenarios.prevention: Proactive mid-stay check-ins catch issues before they become review threats.

difficultGuestScenarios.scenario: Guest reports maintenance failure (AC, hot water, WiFi)
difficultGuestScenarios.response: Acknowledge within 15 minutes. Provide temporary workaround (fans, towels, mobile hotspot). Dispatch maintenance with clear ETA. Offer compensation proportional to disruption. Follow up after fix.
difficultGuestScenarios.prevention: Monthly preventive maintenance checks on all systems. Backup internet connection. Spare parts inventory.

difficultGuestScenarios.response: Respond immediately via WhatsApp/phone. Talk through smart lock troubleshooting. If unresolved in 10 minutes, dispatch local team with backup access. Apologize for inconvenience.
difficultGuestScenarios.prevention: Smart locks with temporary codes. Backup physical key with trusted neighbor or local team. Clear lock instructions in check-in guide.

difficultGuestScenarios.scenario: Guest wants to extend stay but property is booked
difficultGuestScenarios.response: Thank them enthusiastically (great sign!). Check other properties in portfolio. Offer to rebook for future dates with return guest discount. Suggest similar properties if available.
difficultGuestScenarios.prevention: Build multi-property portfolio. Return guest program with 10% discount.

difficultGuestScenarios.scenario: Guest reports feeling unsafe (break-in attempt, suspicious activity)
difficultGuestScenarios.response: Take every report seriously. Contact Tourist Police immediately (+230 number). Offer to relocate guest to alternative accommodation. Stay in contact until resolved. File formal incident report.
difficultGuestScenarios.prevention: Smart locks, motion-activated lighting, security cameras (exterior only), alarm system.

difficultGuestScenarios.response: Send detailed safety briefing 72 hours before arrival if warning active. Include property-specific cyclone preparation steps. Emergency supply locations. 24/7 contact available. Offer flexible rebooking if Class III/IV warning.
difficultGuestScenarios.prevention: Cyclone season (Nov-Apr) booking confirmations include emergency protocols. Emergency supply kits stocked per property.

difficultGuestScenarios.scenario: Guest leaves negative review despite good experience signals
difficultGuestScenarios.response: Respond publicly with professionalism and grace. Address specific concerns factually. Thank them for feedback. Highlight improvements made. Do not be defensive or argue.
difficultGuestScenarios.prevention: Pre-departure message asking for honest feedback privately first. Address issues before checkout.

commonCorrectionPatterns.mistake: Generic copy-paste responses that don't reference the guest's specific situation
commonCorrectionPatterns.correction: Always personalize: mention guest name, dates, property name, specific question asked
commonCorrectionPatterns.why: Guests immediately detect template responses - kills trust and premium positioning

commonCorrectionPatterns.mistake: Over-promising on timing (We'll fix it right away!)
commonCorrectionPatterns.correction: Give honest ETAs with buffer. Under-promise, over-deliver. 'Our team will be there within 2 hours' when you expect 1 hour.
commonCorrectionPatterns.why: Broken promises destroy trust faster than slow service

commonCorrectionPatterns.mistake: Ignoring the emotional component of complaints
commonCorrectionPatterns.correction: Acknowledge the feeling before jumping to the solution. 'I completely understand how frustrating that must be' before 'Here's what we're doing about it'
commonCorrectionPatterns.why: Guests need to feel heard, not just serviced

commonCorrectionPatterns.mistake: Sending check-in instructions too early or too late
commonCorrectionPatterns.correction: 48 hours before arrival is the sweet spot. Too early = forgotten. Too late = anxiety.
commonCorrectionPatterns.why: Timing affects whether guests actually read and follow instructions

commonCorrectionPatterns.mistake: Not following up after resolving an issue
commonCorrectionPatterns.correction: Always close the loop: 'Just checking - is everything working well now?' within 2-4 hours of fix
commonCorrectionPatterns.why: Follow-up turns a negative into a positive review mention

commonCorrectionPatterns.mistake: Using informal tone with cultures that expect formality (and vice versa)
commonCorrectionPatterns.correction: Match the guest's communication style. French guests get 'vous' until they switch to 'tu'. German guests get precise, detailed responses.
commonCorrectionPatterns.why: Cultural mismatch undermines the premium experience you're selling

commonCorrectionPatterns.correction: Limit to: confirmation, pre-arrival (48h), welcome (arrival day), mid-stay check (3+ nights), pre-departure, review request. No daily messages.
commonCorrectionPatterns.why: Excessive messaging feels intrusive and gets guests to tune out important info

commonCorrectionPatterns.mistake: Not mentioning the tourist tax upfront
commonCorrectionPatterns.correction: Include tourist tax (EUR 3/person/night, 12+) in booking confirmation. No surprises at check-in.
commonCorrectionPatterns.why: Undisclosed fees trigger instant negative reviews

contents: Full address with Google Maps link
Check-in time window (3-8 PM) and access instructions

welcomeDay.timing: Check-in day, sent by 3 PM or upon confirmed arrival
contents: Tonight's dinner recommendations (2-3 options with distance)
Quick cultural tip or local insight
This week's local events or highlights
Late checkout option if available (MUR 500 until 2 PM)
Direct review link for the booking platform used

automatedMessaging.automationRate: 75% of routine communications can be automated
automatedMessaging.humanOverride: Complex issues, complaints, emergencies, and culturally sensitive situations always require human response

fiveStageProtocol: Assess: Gather all relevant information from guest
Act: Implement solution with clear timeline communicated
Analyze: Document for prevention and insurance

cycloneClassifications.classI: 36-48 hours warning - prepare supplies, secure property, notify guests
cycloneClassifications.classII: 12-24 hours warning - complete preparations, detailed guest communication
cycloneClassifications.classIII: 6-12 hours warning - final preparations, shelter in place instructions
cycloneClassifications.classIV: Immediate danger - emergency shelter, no movement permitted

delightFactors: Personalized welcome messages referencing their occasion (birthday, anniversary, honeymoon)
Insider local recommendations beyond tourist guides
Surprise amenities (local fruits, personalized welcome note)
Memorable cultural connections (market tour tips, cooking class referrals)

compensationGuidelines._note: Friday-specific bands per refund-metrics.json take precedence over generic STR industry rates below
compensationGuidelines.minorIssue: Up to 5% of nightly rate per affected night (per refund-metrics.json)
compensationGuidelines.moderateIssue: 10-30% of nightly rate per affected night (per refund-metrics.json)
compensationGuidelines.severeIssue: 100% of affected nights' rate + relocation if needed (per refund-metrics.json)
compensationGuidelines.safetyIssue: Immediate relocation + full refund + additional compensation

protocol: Listen actively - understand the full concern
Apologize sincerely - take responsibility regardless of cause
Compensate fairly - match compensation to actual impact
Follow up - confirm satisfaction within 2-4 hours

classI.action: Prepare supplies, secure property, notify guests

Emergency supply kit (3 days water, food, flashlights, batteries, radio, first aid, cash)

response: Evacuate if water levels pose danger

prevention: Interconnected smoke alarms in all rooms
response: Use extinguishers only if safe and trained

noiseAndNeighbors.quietHours: 10 PM - 8 AM (standard, adjust per local rules)
noiseAndNeighbors.monitoring: Privacy-compliant noise monitors (Minut, NoiseAware, Roomonitor) - decibel only, no recording
preventionMeasures: Clear noise policy in house rules and welcome materials
Automated alerts when noise thresholds exceeded
escalationProcess: First alert: Friendly automated message reminding of quiet hours
Second alert: Personal call/message from host with firm reminder
Third alert: Final warning with consequences stated
Continued violation: Contact authorities if needed, document for platform
noiseAndNeighbors.neighborRelations: Maintain positive relationships with neighbors - they are early warning systems and community ambassadors

strategy.reviewConversion: Target 50%+ of guests leaving reviews (vs 20-30% local average)
strategy.responsePolicy: Respond professionally to ALL reviews - positive and negative

gettingReviews: Pre-departure hint: 'We'd love to hear about your experience'
Post-stay (24h): Direct review link for the specific platform used
Make it easy: One-click direct link, not generic 'leave a review'
Timing matters: 24 hours post-checkout when memories are fresh
respondingToNegative: Wait 24 hours before responding (avoid emotional reaction)
Thank the guest for their feedback
Address specific concerns factually without being defensive
Explain what you've done to prevent recurrence
Invite them back to experience the improvement
Keep it concise - long responses look desperate
respondingToPositive: Reference something unique about their stay
Invite them back with return guest benefits
